skill.name: wiki-qa
skill.description: Answers questions about a code repository using source file analysis. Use when the user asks a question about how something works, wants to understand a component, or needs help navigating the code...
skill.summary: Answers repository questions grounded entirely in source code evidence with inline citations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 4 potential issues that are all false positives upon manual review. The skill is a legitimate documentation/Q&A tool that reads source code files and answers questions based on file content. No actual code execution, external commands, or cryptographic operations are present.

content.user_title: Get instant answers about your codebase
content.value_statement: Stop hunting through files to understand how your code works. This skill reads your source code and provides evidence-based answers with inline citations to specific files and line numbers.
actual_capabilities: Search codebase files to answer questions about implementation details
Provide inline citations linking answers to specific source files and line numbers
Detect question language and respond in the same language
Create Key Files tables mapping files to their roles in the codebase
Synthesize answers from multiple source files for comprehensive understanding
Identify when information is insufficient and suggest files to examine
limitations: Only answers questions based on existing source files, cannot access external documentation
Does not execute code or test functionality, only analyzes static text
Cannot answer questions about runtime behavior or system dependencies
Limited to files present in the repository at analysis time

use_cases.title: New developer onboarding
use_cases.description: Quickly understand how specific components work by asking natural language questions about the codebase structure and implementation.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers joining a new project

use_cases.title: Legacy code investigation
use_cases.description: Navigate unfamiliar codebases efficiently by asking where features are defined and how different modules interact.

use_cases.title: Architecture documentation
use_cases.description: Generate explanations of system architecture and component relationships based on actual source code evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Basic code question
prompt_templates.prompt: How does authentication work in this project?
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a high-level explanation of a specific feature or component

prompt_templates.title: Find implementation location
prompt_templates.prompt: Where is the user validation logic defined?
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to locate specific functions or classes in the codebase

prompt_templates.title: Understand component relationships
prompt_templates.prompt: How do the frontend and backend communicate in this application?
prompt_templates.scenario: When exploring interactions between different parts of the system

prompt_templates.title: Debugging context
prompt_templates.prompt: What could cause the payment processing to fail based on the error handling code?
prompt_templates.scenario: When investigating issues and need to understand error flows and edge cases

output_examples.input: How does the data validation work?
output_examples.output:
Data validation is handled through a multi-layer approach:

1. **Schema Validation** - Input is validated against JSON schemas (src/validators/schema.ts:15-42)
2. **Business Rules** - Custom validators check domain constraints (src/validators/business.ts:8-25)
3. **Sanitization** - User input is sanitized before storage (src/utils/sanitize.ts:12-18)

Key Files:
| File | Role |
|------|------|
| src/validators/schema.ts | Schema definition and validation |
| src/validators/business.ts | Domain-specific rules |
| src/utils/sanitize.ts | Input sanitization |

output_examples.input: Where is the API client configured?
output_examples.output:
The API client is configured in `src/api/client.ts:22-45`. It uses:

- Base URL from environment variables (line 28)
- Timeout of 30 seconds (line 32)
- Retry logic for failed requests (lines 38-42)

The client is initialized in `src/app.ts:15` and passed to services via dependency injection.

output_examples.input: What happens when a user uploads a file?
output_examples.output:
File upload follows this workflow:

1. **Request received** - Router routes POST /upload to handler (src/routes/upload.ts:12)
2. **Validation** - File size and type checked (src/middleware/validate.ts:45-52)
3. **Storage** - File saved to temp directory (src/services/storage.ts:78-92)
4. **Processing** - Background job processes file (src/jobs/process.ts:15-30)

Note: Virus scanning is mentioned in comments but not implemented (src/services/storage.ts:85).

best_practices: Ask specific questions about particular components, functions, or features for the most detailed responses
Review the Key Files table to understand which files are relevant to your question
Follow the inline citations to dive deeper into specific implementation details
Use follow-up questions to explore related components or understand how different parts connect
anti_patterns: Asking questions about topics unrelated to the codebase (this skill only analyzes repository files)
Expecting runtime behavior analysis without looking at test files or execution traces
Assuming answers include information from external documentation or APIs
Requesting code execution or testing functionality (this is read-only analysis)

faq.question: What types of questions can this skill answer?
faq.answer: This skill answers questions about implementation details, file locations, component relationships, and how specific features work based on the source code in your repository.

faq.question: Does this skill execute my code or run tests?
faq.answer: No, this skill only reads and analyzes source files. It does not execute code, run tests, or access runtime behavior.

faq.question: How accurate are the answers?
faq.answer: Answers are grounded entirely in source code evidence with inline citations. The skill will tell you when information is insufficient rather than guessing.

faq.question: Can this skill understand code in any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill analyzes code as text and can work with any programming language present in your repository.

faq.question: What if the skill cannot find the answer?
faq.answer: The skill will explicitly state when information is insufficient and suggest specific files to examine for more details.

faq.question: Does this skill access external documentation or APIs?
faq.answer: No, this skill only analyzes files within your repository. It does not access external documentation, APIs, or online resources.
